Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Post Corporation Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Post Corporation Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Post Corporation Act to

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide that the Corporation must not refuse to provide a postal service within Canada for the interprovincial collection, transmission and delivery â direct to consumers â of beer, wine or spirits;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: give the Corporation the sole and exclusive privilege, within Canada, of collecting, transmitting and delivering interprovincially â direct to consumers â beer, wine or spirits;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: create an exception to this privilege for trusted carriers;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: authorize the Governor in Council to make regulations respecting trusted carriers; and
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Canada Post Corporation Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: beerâhas the same meaning as in section 4 of the Excise Act;â(biÃ¨re)
spiritsâhas the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;â(spiritueux)
wineâhas the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;â(vin)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:
The Corporation shall not refuse to provide a postal service within Canada for the interprovincial collection, transmission and delivery â direct to consumers â of beer, wine or spirits.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 14(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to section 15, the Corporation has, within Canada, the sole and exclusive privilege of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: collecting, transmitting and delivering letters to the addressee thereof; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: collecting, transmitting and delivering interprovincially â direct to consumers â beer, wine and spirits.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 14(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to sections 15 and 15.1, the Corporation has, within Canada, the sole and exclusive privilege of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: collecting, transmitting and delivering letters to the addressee thereof; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: collecting, transmitting and delivering interprovincially â direct to consumers â beer, wine and spirits.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 15(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The exclusive privilege referred to in paragraph 14(1)(a) does not apply to
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 15:

Section.MarginalNote: Exception â beer, wine and spirits
Section.Text: The exclusive privilege referred to in paragraph 14(1)(b) does not apply to beer, wine or spirits collected, transmitted or delivered by a carrier designated as a trusted carrier under this Act.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 20:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting trusted carriers, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establishing processes and criteria for the designation of trusted carriers, such as criteria in respect of age verification protocols and secure handling and delivery procedures;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: designating a common carrier or class of common carriers as a trusted carrier; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the interprovincial collection, transmission and delivery â direct to consumers â of beer, wine and spirits by trusted carriers, including oversight mechanisms.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must cause to be published on the Ministerâs departmental website a list of carriers designated as trusted carriers and must update the list within 30 days after the day on which a carrier is designated as a trusted carrier or loses their designation.
The Minister may, in the absence of regulations, designate a common carrier or class of common carriers as trusted carriers.
Sections 1 and 2, subsection 3(1) and section 6 come into force three months after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Subsection 3(2) and sections 4 and 5 come into force one year after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
